page: Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 is a storage-optimized server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 12 vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU runs at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes a massive 8639 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression also show strong results. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls in the average range. Memory bandwidth tests reveal average performance for small cached reads and large uncached writes, though large uncached read bandwidth is relatively weak.

The L12aos_v4 offers a high resource density of local storage and memory relative to its 12 vCPUs, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring large local datasets without needing massive parallel compute. However, the weak uncached large-block read memory bandwidth presents a potential bottleneck for memory-bound applications with large working sets. This server is highly suited for database hosting, local storage-heavy applications, text processing, and moderate-scale web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 is a storage-focused virtual machine configured with 12 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 6 physical cores with multithreading and a dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with a substantial 8639 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU capabilities and text processing speeds, while multi-core operations and memory write bandwidth perform in the average tier. However, uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the lower percentile. Given its massive local storage capacity and balanced memory-to-core ratio, the L12aos_v4 is highly suited for database operations, storage-intensive workloads, and text processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 is a storage-optimized server featuring 12 vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and 8639 GB of local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor for database and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L12aos_v4 is a storage-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 8.6 TB local storage powered by an AMD EPYC CPU.
tagline: A storage-heavy virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, massive local capacity, and balanced memory for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 12-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM and a massive 8639 GB of local storage.
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance.
Features Premium Storage capability under Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization.
Best suited for database operations, local storage-heavy tasks, and text processing.
